articles.headline: Industries Most Affected by AI in 2026: How Artificial Intelligence Is Changing Work
articles.summary: AI is transforming IT, retail, manufacturing, and logistics through automation of coding, inventory forecasting, predictive maintenance, and route optimization, boosting productivity while reshaping jobs. Businesses from SMEs to corporations can leverage these tools to cut repetitive tasks, improve efficiency, and manage workforce transitions. Adoption varies by sector, with strong impacts on operational costs and competitiveness.

articles.headline: Online Learning Platform Canvas Hacked in Massive Cyberattack
articles.summary: Canvas, serving 30 million students across thousands of schools and universities, suffered a major cyberattack, exposing cybersecurity risks in edtech platforms used by businesses for training. Enterprises and SMEs evaluating digital learning tools must prioritize robust security to mitigate disruptions and compliance issues. This incident highlights vulnerabilities in widely adopted SaaS platforms.

articles.headline: Arm Holdings Publishes Full-Year Fiscal 2026 Results
articles.summary: Arm Holdings released full-year FY2026 results, offering critical insights into chip licensing trends, AI compute exposure, and the semiconductor IP cycle that impact hardware design costs and supply chains for tech firms. Businesses reliant on Arm-based processors can assess ongoing demand and innovation pace in mobile, edge, and data center chips. This data influences strategic planning for AI infrastructure investments.
